rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO ADVISORS LLC VS. DERON A. KARTOON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 14, 2013, Line 6, PETITIONER WELLS FARGO ADVISORS LLC's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. The arbitration award is confirmed, as follows: $687,559.10 which is the Arbitration amount, $10,500.53 for attorneys' fees awarded by the arbitrator; interest of $79,653.60 from March 16, 2010 through March 25, 2013 at $72.15 per day; and attorney fees of $821.50 incurred in bringing this Petition. The request for costs is denied without prejudice; there was no evidence of costs submitted with the moving papers. No opposition was filed. The prevailing party is to prepare a proposed Order and a separate proposed Judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. RMF ENGINEERING, INC., A MARYLAND CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Motion To Appoint The Honorable Ronald M Sabraw (Ret) As Special Master
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 14, 2013, line 1, PLAINTIFF THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, Joinder In Motion To Appoint The Honorable Ronald M Sabraw (Ret) As Special Master, Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. IT APPEARS FROM THE PAPERS THAT THE APPOINTMENT OF A SPECIAL MASTER WOULD BE APPROPRIATE IN THIS CASE. HOWEVER, COUNSEL SHOULD BE PREPARED TO RESPOND TO THE FOLLOWING CONCERNING THE PROPOSED ORDER: (1) ARE THE PROVISIONS OF PARAGRAPHS 5,7&8 OF THE PROPOSED ORDER WITHIN THE PROVISIONS OF CCP SEC. 639(a)5? (2) ISN'T THERE A REQUIREMENT TO STATE WITH SPECIFICITY THE CIRCUMSTANCES WHICH BRINGS CCP SEC 639(a)5 INTO PLAY? (3) WHAT IS THE MEANING OF THE LANGUAGE ON LINE 27 OF PARAGRAPH 5 WHICH READS "WITH FULL AUTHORITY TO SETTLE, AND/OR CONSULTANTS" Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAFEWAY INC. VS. MCKESSON CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 14, 2013, line 4, PLAINTIFF SAFEWAY INC.'S Motion To Compel Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF SAFEWAY, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS, SET ON AND TWO IS GRANTED SUBJECT TO THE PROVISIONS OF THE PROTECTIVE ORDER AGREED TO BETWEEN THE PARTIES. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

Overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer is overruled as to the second and fourth causes of action. These causes of action are properly pled. Plaintiff is not entitled to damages or penalties for misclassification under Labor Code section 226.8 but is given leave to amend to plead an alternative basis for recovery. rulings.Case Title: ALICIA P. VICTORIO et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF S.F.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 14, 2013, Line 18, PLAINTIFFs ALICIA VICTORIO and CONRADO VICTORIO's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Complaint. Continued on the court's own motion to June 12, 2013 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 14, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
